manifest.name: Scholar Quest

name: Scholar Quest

releases.author: Zhao-Jun-Yong
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

Five XP re-award and snapshot corruption bugs, all sharing the same root cause: startup metadata events (Obsidian re-indexing the vault on launch) were indistinguishable from live user edits, so XP was awarded again for historical activity on every reload.

**What was happening:** On startup, Obsidian fires `metadata:changed` and `vault:create` events for every file in the vault. The plugin had no way to tell these apart from genuine user edits, so it re-awarded paper XP, atomic-note development XP, and writing progress XP on every reload — flooding the 500-entry activity log and pushing out the career-init entry.

**The fix:** All three XP-awarding paths now check `file.stat.mtime >= loadedAt`. If the file was last modified before the plugin loaded, no XP is awarded — the snapshot is updated and guards are bootstrapped silently instead.

### Fixes

- **Sources** (`paper-skimmed` / `paper-completed`): paper XP re-awarded on every Obsidian reload when snapshot keywords were stale
- **Ideas** (`atomic-note-developed`): development XP re-awarded on startup for notes edited between sessions; 60-min cooldown always expired after a restart
- **Projects** (`writing-progress`): writing XP re-awarded on startup for project files edited between sessions; `peakWordCount` also advanced silently, shifting the threshold
- **`onFileCreate` snapshot wipe**: Obsidian fires `create` events for all pre-existing files during startup indexing; the handler was overwriting every snapshot unconditionally, erasing `completedAt`/`skimmedAt`, `lastDevelopmentAt`, `peakWordCount`, and daily writing state — the source of missing guards that let the above bugs fire
- **Career calibration double-XP**: re-opening the career wizard searched the activity log for the previous `career-init` entry to subtract old XP before adding the new value; if the log had overflowed the entry was missing, old XP was not subtracted, and new XP stacked on top — fixed by deriving `prevXP` from the stored `careerData` object via the new `calcCareerXP()` function

### Tests

87 tests (9 new covering startup-guard behaviour).
releases.name: 1.5.0 — Fix XP re-award on reload

releases.author: Zhao-Jun-Yong
releases.description:
## What's new

### Manual log — activity catalog
The free-text "Add activity" input is replaced by a searchable catalog of **52 built-in activities** across 10 discipline categories: wet lab, computational, fieldwork, teaching, mentoring, service, outreach, clinical/participant research, literature work, and off-vault writing. Build a personal shortlist by adding from the catalog; the log modal shows only your list. A fixed **Other (+30 XP, once/day)** entry is always available as a catch-all. The catalog is collapsed by default in settings.

### XP integrity
- **Reading tag one-time guard** — paper-skimmed and paper-completed XP are awarded once per file. Removing and re-adding the ✅ tag no longer re-awards XP.
- **Atomic note diminishing returns** — notes 1–10 per day earn full XP (30); notes 11–30 earn half (15); notes 31+ earn a trickle (5). Heavy knowledge-work days are still rewarded; bulk farming is not.
- **Milestone XP locked** — built-in milestone XP values are now fixed and shown as read-only. User-added custom milestones are capped at 200 XP.
- **XP values removed from settings** — auto-detected XP values (paper, writing, notes) are no longer user-editable, keeping progression meaningful.

### Runtime improvements
- Per-file 300ms debounce on metadata change events — prevents race conditions when files are edited rapidly
- Metadata events skipped during initial vault indexing — no redundant reads or sidebar renders on startup
- Sidebar re-render debounced at 400ms — eliminates jank during active writing sessions
- Project milestone initialisation batched to a single save on startup

## Upgrading
Existing data is automatically migrated — no action needed. Built-in milestone XP values are reset to defaults on first load.
releases.name: v1.2.0 — XP integrity, activity catalog, runtime improvements

releases.author: Zhao-Jun-Yong
releases.description: ## What's new ### Improvements for first-time users - README completely overhauled: corrected tier/level counts (12 tiers, max level 60), added Getting Started, Recommended Workflow, Tier Progression, and Achievements sections - Roadmap simplified to plain bullet points ### Status bar - Replaced ⚗️ emoji with a pixel art scroll icon rendered as an `` element ### Achievement system - 5 achievements now auto-unlock when career history is imported: **Graduate!**, **On Stage**, **Funded!**, **Under Review**, **Accepted!** - Renamed **Doctor!** → **Graduate!** to cover both Masters and PhD thesis defences - 7 achievement badge sprites scaled up for visual consistency ## Installation Download `main.js` and `manifest.json` below and copy to `.obsidian/plugins/scholar-quest/` in your vault.
releases.name: 0.2.0 — Pixel art scroll, achievement fixes, first-time user docs

releases.author: Zhao-Jun-Yong
releases.description:
## Scholar Quest 0.1.0 — Initial Release

Gamify your academic workflow with XP, levels, and milestone tracking.

### Features
- **Auto-detected XP** — papers read, atomic notes created/developed, writing progress
- **Milestone system** — 14 project types with predefined milestone templates
- **Manual log** — log off-Obsidian activities in ~2 seconds
- **Level system** — 12 tiers (Dormant egg → Nova dragon), levels 1–60
- **Achievement badges** — 26 pixel art achievement icons
- **Status bar** — ambient level indicator
- **Fully configurable** — vault paths, tags, XP values, milestone templates

### Installation
1. Download `main.js` and `manifest.json` below
2. Create `.obsidian/plugins/scholar-quest/` in your vault
3. Copy both files in and enable the plugin in Settings → Community plugins

---
☕ [Buy me a coffee](https://buymeacoffee.com/zhaojun0425) if Scholar Quest helps your research!
releases.name: Scholar Quest 0.1.0
